mechanism_narrative: Robust retail sales growth indicates strong consumer demand, which directly translates to higher corporate revenues and earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short horizon.
failure_condition: If RSAFS 3-month average growth is positive but SPX 5-day returns are negative in more than 55% of observations over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained increase in the unemployment rate, especially after a period of low unemployment, signals an impending economic slowdown or recession, leading to negative S&P 500 returns as corporate profits are expected to decline.
failure_condition: If SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 40% of instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate is elevated and retail sales growth is simultaneously slowing, it indicates that tight monetary policy is effectively curbing demand, which will negatively impact corporate earnings and lead to lower S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: If SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of periods where FEDFUNDS is above 4.0% and RSAFS 3-month growth is negative.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of economic recovery, increasing industrial production signals a rebound in manufacturing and overall economic activity, which supports higher corporate valuations and positive S&P 500 returns over a longer horizon.
failure_condition: If INDPRO growth is positive during a recovery but SPX 60-day returns are negative in more than 50% of such triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent job growth, as measured by non-farm payrolls, reflects a healthy labor market and strong consumer spending potential, which underpins corporate profitability and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS 3-month average growth is positive but SPX 20-day returns are negative in more than 55% of observations over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant drop in the inflation rate (CPIAUCSL) below a certain threshold signals disinflationary pressures, which can lead the central bank to ease monetary policy, boosting equity valuations and S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: If SPX 5-day returns are negative in more than 50% of instances where CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change falls below 2.0%.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts are declining while the Federal Funds Rate is rising, it indicates that higher interest rates are severely impacting interest-sensitive sectors like housing, signaling a broader economic contraction and negative S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month average < 1000k units and FEDFUNDS 3-month change > 0.25%
failure_condition: If SPX 60-day returns are positive in more than 45% of periods where HOUST is below 1000k units and FEDFUNDS has risen by more than 0.25% over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of strong economic growth (high GDP), robust retail sales growth confirms strong consumer confidence and spending, which directly translates to higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: If SPX 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of instances where GDPC1 growth is above 3.0% and RSAFS growth is also positive.
